name: Spain (civil flag)

colors.symbolism: from the historic arms of Castile and Aragon
the gold of Castile and the Aragonese pales

description: A horizontal triband of red, yellow and red, the central yellow stripe twice as wide as each outer red stripe; the civil version omits the national coat of arms.
history: The red-and-yellow scheme dates from the naval ensign approved by Charles III in 1785. The civil version (without the coat of arms) was traditionally used by private citizens; the current Constitution (1978) and Law 39/1981 confirm the design but in practice only the version with the arms is now displayed officially.

features.symbolism: the rojigualda; the gold and red of the historic arms of the Crown of Castile and the Crown of Aragon
